topic: Hot Desert Systems & Landscapes / Coastal Systems & Landscapes / Glacial Systems & Landscapes
subject: geography

items.correct: The area of land drained by a river and its tributaries, bounded by a watershed (high ground that separates one basin from another).
items.misconception: A drainage basin is the bowl-shaped area at the mouth of the river.
items.random_wrong: A drainage basin is the underground source of a river.
items.why: Students take 'basin' too literally as a bowl. It is the entire catchment area — often a huge region, not a depression near the river itself.

items.q: Why is the Demographic Transition Model criticised for not applying to all countries?
items.correct: It was based on European industrialisation. LICs today face different conditions: imported medicine without industrialisation, cultural factors, gender inequality and globalised economies — not all follow the same path.
items.misconception: All countries follow the five stages of the DTM in order.
items.random_wrong: The DTM only applies to countries with more than 50 million people.
items.why: Many LICs experienced rapid death rate falls without industrialisation, breaking the model's logic. Some HICs are entering a Stage 5 it didn't predict — the DTM is a starting point, not a universal law.

items.q: What is the ITCZ and why does it bring rain?
items.correct: The Intertropical Convergence Zone is where trade winds from both hemispheres converge near the equator, forcing air to rise, cool and produce heavy convectional rainfall.
items.misconception: The ITCZ is a fixed band of permanent storms at the equator.
items.random_wrong: The ITCZ is the boundary between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans.
items.why: The ITCZ shifts seasonally north and south with the sun, driving monsoon rains. Students treat it as static — its movement explains why tropical regions have wet/dry seasons.

items.q: Which glacier type erodes most actively?
items.why: Correct answer: Warm-based. “Cold-based” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Sheep rock. “Ice mountain” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Antarctic contribution to sea level rise in mm/year?
items.why: Exact answer: 3 mm/year. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
